summary:
Only include content from Agent1’s response; do NOT add external knowledge or interpretation.

A 25-year-old male with a penetrating chest trauma and active bleeding into the pericardium due to a rupture of the right ventricle shows signs of severe hypovolemic shock requiring immediate intervention for life-threatening hemorrhage. Definitive treatment should involve direct suture closure of the myocaridl defect using interrupted 2–0 polypropylene sutures with pledgets as this provides stable hemostasis while preserving heart function post-repair.
